disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No vesicular (blister) prodrome; ulcers only on non-keratinised mucosa (cheeks/lip), sparing gums/hard palate/tongue
trigger_question_examples: Did little blisters come before the ulcers, and are the gums or roof of the mouth involved?
Are the ulcers on the soft cheek/lip lining or on the firm gums/palate?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No blisters first, and it's the soft lining of my cheeks and lip — my gums and the roof of my mouth are clear.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Reduced fluid intake with dehydration risk — poor urine output, lightheadedness (living alone)
trigger_question_examples: How much have you managed to drink and pass urine today?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I've barely drunk anything and not passed much urine; I feel a bit lightheaded standing.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No spread to lips/skin, no fever/malaise, no lymphadenopathy; ulcers <3 weeks and recurrent pattern
trigger_question_examples: Any fever, spreading to the outside of your lips, or swollen neck glands?
Have any single ulcers lasted more than 3 weeks?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No fever, nothing on the outside of my lips, no swollen glands, and they always heal within a couple of weeks.
